tossups.question: In a poem titled for this character, the speaker says, “At eve a dry cicala sung, / There came a sound as of the sea” as “Heaven over Heaven rose the night.” In that poem, this character kneels and “on the liquid mirror glow'd / The clear perfection of her face.” This character’s tears “fell with the dews at even” and “fell ere the dews were dried” in a poem where “Old footsteps trod the upper floors” and “Old voices called her from without.” The speaker describes how this character moans “Aye Mary” and sings about being “all alone, / To live forgotten, and love forlorn” in a poem titled for this character (*) “in the South.” The author of that poem wrote a poem where this character says, “My life is dreary, / He cometh not.” A line about this character “in the moated grange” serves as the epigraph to a poem where this character repeats, “‘I am aweary, aweary, / I would that I were dead!’” For 10 points, name this subject of two Tennyson poems, who pursues Angelo in the play Measure for Measure.
tossups.question_sanitized: In a poem titled for this character, the speaker says, "At eve a dry cicala sung, / There came a sound as of the sea" as "Heaven over Heaven rose the night." In that poem, this character kneels and "on the liquid mirror glow'd / The clear perfection of her face." This character's tears "fell with the dews at even" and "fell ere the dews were dried" in a poem where "Old footsteps trod the upper floors" and "Old voices called her from without." The speaker describes how this character moans "Aye Mary" and sings about being "all alone, / To live forgotten, and love forlorn" in a poem titled for this character (*) "in the South." The author of that poem wrote a poem where this character says, "My life is dreary, / He cometh not." A line about this character "in the moated grange" serves as the epigraph to a poem where this character repeats, "'I am aweary, aweary, / I would that I were dead!'" For 10 points, name this subject of two Tennyson poems, who pursues Angelo in the play Measure for Measure.

tossups.question: This feature’s transverse northern border contains the remains of a volcanic plateau known as Dutton Ridge. Its topography includes Eifuku (ay-FOO-koo), a liquid carbon dioxide spewing volcano known as a Champagne Vent, and the molten sulfur pools of Daikoku, which prior to their discovery only were known from Io. An eponymous microbiota forms the bottom of a food chain which also contains the xenophyophores, many of whom dwell in its Sirena Deep. This home of the class monothalamea (mo-no-tha-la-MAY-a) is continuous with features named Bonin and Izu to its north, and its lowest point was first visited by Jacques Picard and Don Walsh in 1960 using the Bathyscaphe (BA-thi-skayf) Trieste. For 10 points, name this trench east of the Philippine Sea, the home of the Challenger Deep, the deepest portion of the Pacific Ocean.
tossups.question_sanitized: This feature's transverse northern border contains the remains of a volcanic plateau known as Dutton Ridge. Its topography includes Eifuku (ay-FOO-koo), a liquid carbon dioxide spewing volcano known as a Champagne Vent, and the molten sulfur pools of Daikoku, which prior to their discovery only were known from Io. An eponymous microbiota forms the bottom of a food chain which also contains the xenophyophores, many of whom dwell in its Sirena Deep. This home of the class monothalamea (mo-no-tha-la-MAY-a) is continuous with features named Bonin and Izu to its north, and its lowest point was first visited by Jacques Picard and Don Walsh in 1960 using the Bathyscaphe (BA-thi-skayf) Trieste. For 10 points, name this trench east of the Philippine Sea, the home of the Challenger Deep, the deepest portion of the Pacific Ocean.

tossups.question: Fuel shortages on one side in this battle led to them using an especially flammable type of oil that contributed to their high casualty rate. This lack of fuel also led a battle plan, called Operation A-Go, to be drawn up intended for a fight much further to the west. At the end of this battle, at least 88 planes fell into the ocean after running out of fuel, but most pilots were saved when Marc Mitscher made the risky move of turning on the lights on his ships. One ship was sunk during this battle by a single torpedo from the Albacore and another was sunk by the Cavalla -these were the carriers Taiho and Shokaku. This battle began when the Combined Fleet was intercepted as it attempted to relieve the land forces at the contemporaneous battle of Saipan, and the ease with which Raymond Spruance's 5th Fleet gunned down nearly 600 planes in this battle led to its famous nickname. For 10 points, name this June 1944 carrier battle between the US and Japan that was fought in a body of water northeast of Manila.
tossups.answer: Battle of the Philippine Sea or the Great Marianas Turkey Shoot
tossups.answer_sanitized: Battle of the Philippine Sea or the Great Marianas Turkey Shoot
tossups.question_sanitized: Fuel shortages on one side in this battle led to them using an especially flammable type of oil that contributed to their high casualty rate. This lack of fuel also led a battle plan, called Operation A-Go, to be drawn up intended for a fight much further to the west. At the end of this battle, at least 88 planes fell into the ocean after running out of fuel, but most pilots were saved when Marc Mitscher made the risky move of turning on the lights on his ships. One ship was sunk during this battle by a single torpedo from the Albacore and another was sunk by the Cavalla -these were the carriers Taiho and Shokaku. This battle began when the Combined Fleet was intercepted as it attempted to relieve the land forces at the contemporaneous battle of Saipan, and the ease with which Raymond Spruance's 5th Fleet gunned down nearly 600 planes in this battle led to its famous nickname. For 10 points, name this June 1944 carrier battle between the US and Japan that was fought in a body of water northeast of Manila.

bonuses.leadin: Megathrust earthquakes in these zones are among the factors against proposals to dispose of nuclear waste there. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Megathrust earthquakes in these zones are among the factors against proposals to dispose of nuclear waste there. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this deepest of the pelagic ocean zones which encompasses trenches.
The goal of burying nuclear waste in hadal zones is to have this process, which occurs at arc-trench complexes, naturally bury the waste.
Among the hadal zones proposed for nuclear waste disposal is this deepest trench in the Pacific Ocean.
parts_sanitized: Name this deepest of the pelagic ocean zones which encompasses trenches.
The goal of burying nuclear waste in hadal zones is to have this process, which occurs at arc-trench complexes, naturally bury the waste.
Among the hadal zones proposed for nuclear waste disposal is this deepest trench in the Pacific Ocean.

bonuses.leadin: A territory named for this person was consolidated at the expense of the Aesti under Lembitu and the Semigallians under Viestards. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A territory named for this person was consolidated at the expense of the Aesti under Lembitu and the Semigallians under Viestards. For 10 points each:
parts: Albert of Buxhoeveden (“books-HO-vuh-din”) promoted the use of what person’s name for the Sword Brothers’ state in Livonia? John II Casimir appointed this person monarch of Poland via the Lwów Oath.
As part of his campaigns to establish the Terra Mariana in Livonia, Albert founded this future Hanseatic city, a present-day Baltic capital on the Daugava River.

A medieval merchant’s guild based in Riga, the Brotherhood of Blackheads, legendarily adopted Mary as their patron saint after defending Tallinn during this 1343 revolt by indigenous Estonian groups like the Oeselia.
parts_sanitized: Albert of Buxhoeveden ("books-HO-vuh-din") promoted the use of what person's name for the Sword Brothers' state in Livonia? John II Casimir appointed this person monarch of Poland via the Lwow Oath.
As part of his campaigns to establish the Terra Mariana in Livonia, Albert founded this future Hanseatic city, a present-day Baltic capital on the Daugava River.

A medieval merchant's guild based in Riga, the Brotherhood of Blackheads, legendarily adopted Mary as their patron saint after defending Tallinn during this 1343 revolt by indigenous Estonian groups like the Oeselia.
answers_sanitized: Virgin Mary [or the Madonna; accept Terra Mariana]
Saint George's Night Uprising [or Jurioo ulestous]

bonuses.leadin: Victor Vescovo recounted eating a tuna sandwich and observing candy wrappers in this location in a 2019 solo dive as part of the Five Deeps Expedition. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Pacific Ocean trench whose Challenger Deep has the lowest known seafloor depth.

An oceanographer from this family and Don Walsh crewed the Trieste in the first manned trip to reach Challenger Deep. This family of Swiss balloonists includes Bertrand, who achieved the first balloon circumnavigation in 1999 with Brian Jones.

This living fossil has a long snout called a rostrum that senses electrical currents from fish in the Mariana Trench. Japanese fishermen assigned a mythical name to this pinkish shark with an extendable jaw.
answers: Piccard family [accept Jacques Piccard, Jean Piccard, Auguste Piccard, or Bertrand Piccard] (The Star Trek character Jean-Luc Picard was named for them.)

packet.name: Emergency 2. Toronto A, Berkeley B, Yale A

answers_sanitized: Piccard family [accept Jacques Piccard, Jean Piccard, Auguste Piccard, or Bertrand Piccard] (The Star Trek character Jean-Luc Picard was named for them.)
goblin shark [or vampire shark; or Mitsukurina owstoni; accept Mitsukurina]

bonuses.leadin: A book by Roland B. Dixon records a creation myth from this region in which the cosmic spider Areop-Enap squeezes into a mussel shell and creates the world with help from a profusely sweating worm. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this cultural region home to the sky god Luk and his son Olofat, a trickster figure who upsets children by giving spines to the previously-harmless scorpionfish while visiting the heavens.
Olofat’s dual role as hero and trickster parallels that of this figure from Polynesian mythology, who slowed down the sun by beating it with the jawbone of his grandmother.
While in heaven, Olofat altered some docile pets by equipping them with these objects. Māui was killed by obsidian examples of these objects belonging to the night goddess Hine-nui-te-pō.
answers: Micronesia [accept Nauru; accept Caroline Islands or Gilbert Islands; accept Mariana Islands; accept Federated States of Micronesia; accept Pelew Islands; accept Yap; prompt on the Pacific Ocean; prompt on Oceania]
answers_sanitized: Micronesia [accept Nauru; accept Caroline Islands or Gilbert Islands; accept Mariana Islands; accept Federated States of Micronesia; accept Pelew Islands; accept Yap; prompt on the Pacific Ocean; prompt on Oceania]
teeth [or tooth; accept shark teeth; accept vagina dentata]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A book by Roland B. Dixon records a creation myth from this region in which the cosmic spider Areop-Enap squeezes into a mussel shell and creates the world with help from a profusely sweating worm. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this cultural region home to the sky god Luk and his son Olofat, a trickster figure who upsets children by giving spines to the previously-harmless scorpionfish while visiting the heavens.
Olofat's dual role as hero and trickster parallels that of this figure from Polynesian mythology, who slowed down the sun by beating it with the jawbone of his grandmother.
While in heaven, Olofat altered some docile pets by equipping them with these objects. Maui was killed by obsidian examples of these objects belonging to the night goddess Hine-nui-te-po.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the accounts published in Library of America’s World War II Memoirs: The Pacific Theater, for 10 points each.
parts: Samuel Hynes’s Flights of Passage recounts the bloody battle for this island, which Saul David called the “crucible of hell.” Ernie Pyle died on Iejima off this Japanese Ryūkyū island, the site of Kadena Air Base.

E. B. Sledge’s With the Old Breed: At Peleliu and Okinawa depicts the campaign for this archipelago and Palau, which included a “turkey shoot.” 14 islands in this archipelago became an American commonwealth in 1978.

Alvin Kernan’s Crossing the Line recalls landing troops on Makin Atoll during this battle, where Louis Hayward shot a film “with the marines.” A duck served at this Operation Galvanic battle that preceded the Marshalls campaign.
answers: Okinawa [or Uchinaa; or Okinawa-hontō or Okinawa-jima; accept Battle of Okinawa or Okinawa-sen; accept Crucible of Hell: The Heroism and Tragedy of Okinawa]

Mariana Islands [or Manislan Mariånas; accept Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands or CNMI or Sankattan Siha Na Islas Mariånas or Commonwealth Téél Falúw kka Efáng llól Marianas] (Sledge’s book was a major source for Steven Spielberg’s miniseries The Pacific.)
Battle of Tarawa [accept With the Marines at Tarawa]
answers_sanitized: Okinawa [or Uchinaa; or Okinawa-honto or Okinawa-jima; accept Battle of Okinawa or Okinawa-sen; accept Crucible of Hell: The Heroism and Tragedy of Okinawa]

Mariana Islands [or Manislan Marianas; accept Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands or CNMI or Sankattan Siha Na Islas Marianas or Commonwealth Teel Faluw kka Efang llol Marianas] (Sledge's book was a major source for Steven Spielberg's miniseries The Pacific.)
Battle of Tarawa [accept With the Marines at Tarawa]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about the accounts published in Library of America's World War II Memoirs: The Pacific Theater, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: Samuel Hynes's Flights of Passage recounts the bloody battle for this island, which Saul David called the "crucible of hell." Ernie Pyle died on Iejima off this Japanese Ryukyu island, the site of Kadena Air Base.

E. B. Sledge's With the Old Breed: At Peleliu and Okinawa depicts the campaign for this archipelago and Palau, which included a "turkey shoot." 14 islands in this archipelago became an American commonwealth in 1978.

Alvin Kernan's Crossing the Line recalls landing troops on Makin Atoll during this battle, where Louis Hayward shot a film "with the marines." A duck served at this Operation Galvanic battle that preceded the Marshalls campaign.

bonuses.leadin: An engineer is building an underwater tunnel. For 10 points each:
parts: He or she may choose to build an immersible tube, which involves placing prefabricated pipe pieces into a manmade example of these depressions. In geology, oceanic ones of these structures are made by subducting plates.

The components of the immersible tube are likely built with these interior walls, which increase the tunnel's structural integrity and may localize the spread of water in the event of a hull breach. They can also be found in airplane fuselages and ships.
Finally, the engineer may choose to cover the tunnel with rock armor to prevent the tube from undergoing this process, in which weathering degrades it.
answers: trenches [accept Marianas trench; do not accept or prompt on “fault”]
answers_sanitized: trenches [accept Marianas trench; do not accept or prompt on "fault"]

bonuses.leadin: Leading Chilean writers like Enrique Lafourcade attended this author’s fancy salons, which took place at the same time dissidents were tortured in her basement. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author, a secret agent for Pinochet who was married to the assassin Michael Townley. In 2008, she was jailed for her role in the bombing of General Carlos Prats.
This author depicted one of the salons of Mariana Callejas in the final section of his novel By Night in Chile. Hundreds of women in Santa Teresa are mysteriously murdered in his massive novel 2666.

Bolaño’s novel Amulet is based on a 1968 incident in which a poet from this country, Alcira Soust Scaffo, hid for 15 days during the occupation of UNAM. Another author from this country, Eduardo Galeano, wrote Open Veins of Latin America.
answers: Oriental Republic of Uruguay [or República Oriental del Uruguay]
answers_sanitized: Oriental Republic of Uruguay [or Republica Oriental del Uruguay]

parts_sanitized: Name this author, a secret agent for Pinochet who was married to the assassin Michael Townley. In 2008, she was jailed for her role in the bombing of General Carlos Prats.
This author depicted one of the salons of Mariana Callejas in the final section of his novel By Night in Chile. Hundreds of women in Santa Teresa are mysteriously murdered in his massive novel 2666.

Bolano's novel Amulet is based on a 1968 incident in which a poet from this country, Alcira Soust Scaffo, hid for 15 days during the occupation of UNAM. Another author from this country, Eduardo Galeano, wrote Open Veins of Latin America.

bonuses.leadin: Clifford Geertz examined some of these people in Peddlers and Princes. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these people who start-up companies. George W. Bush famously said that the French had no word for these people.
This Austrian economist argued that entrepreneurs were central to business cycles and long term endogenously derived economic growth because they were the agents of “creative destruction”.

This recent economist has developed Schumpeter’s ideas into the concept of the “entrepreneurial state”, arguing that the idea of the monolithic bureaucratic state which only intervenes in market failure is ridiculous as government-funding dependent technology like the iPhone shows.

bonuses.leadin: The unmanned Japanese robotic probe ABISMO visited this place in 2008. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this trench in the Western Pacific Ocean that contains the deepest parts of the world’s oceans.

This deepest part of the Marianas Trench is named after the ship that made the first measurements of its depth in the 19th century. Director James Cameron descended to this place in a similarly named vessel in 2012.

Challenger Deep is located in a country called the “Federated States” of this Western Pacific island region that includes the nations of Palau and Kiribati [“KEER-uh-boss”], as well as the U.S. territory of Guam.

bonuses.leadin: The highest point of this island is Mount Takpochao, and its judiciary meets at Susupe. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this island that is located in a U.S. commonwealth. The site of a fierce World War II battle, many Japanese civilians jumped to their deaths here off this island’s Banzai Cliff.

Saipan is located in this commonwealth of the United States in the western Pacific Ocean. This commonwealth's island of Pagan was evacuated in 1981 after a volcanic eruption, and other islands in this commonwealth include Rota and Tinian.
This Marianas chain also includes this unincorporated U.S. territory with capital at Agana. Its native peoples are the Chamorro.
answers: Northern Mariana Islands [or Northern Marianas; do not accept or prompt on “Marianas”]
answers_sanitized: Northern Mariana Islands [or Northern Marianas; do not accept or prompt on "Marianas"]
